product.name: pi-bun-effect
product.tagline: A Bun-first, Effect-TS agent platform compatible with pi sessions, extensible via packages, skills, templates, and a safer extension system.
product.problem_statement: Current implementations split between a TypeScript/Node monorepo agent stack and a Rust single-binary port. We need a single Bun-native (runtime + ecosystem) implementation using Effect-TS to unify concurrency, error handling, and modular services while preserving pi-mono UX and session semantics.

goals: Preserve pi-mono behavioral contracts: JSONL v3 sessions with tree branching, compaction rules, RPC protocol, skills/templates/packages discovery, event streaming semantics.
Deliver Bun-first performance: fast startup, low memory footprint, responsive TUI, minimal overhead in tool calls and code search.
Adopt Effect-TS for structured concurrency, typed errors, resource scoping, and deterministic testing hooks.
Unify all surfaces: CLI/TUI, RPC, SDK, web UI integration, Slack bot, and vLLM pod manager under a shared core.

must_have: Core types: message/content blocks, usage/cost, events schema (agent/message/tool).
Unified LLM adapter with streaming, partial tool-call JSON support, model registry, provider configuration, token + cost tracking (parity with pi-ai functionality).
Agent runtime: tool calling loop, message queueing (steer/followUp modes), abort/retry, compaction triggers, session persistence.
JSONL v3 session read/write and tree navigation; optional SQLite index cache for query/search.
CLI with modes: interactive TUI, print/JSON, RPC-over-stdio, SDK API.
Built-in tools for coding harness: read/write/edit/bash at minimum; grep/find/ls recommended for parity with existing stacks.
Extensions API: register tools/commands/shortcuts/flags, event interception, UI prompts; package manager for npm/git/path sources; hot reload where safe.
Skills + prompt templates + themes discovery and parsing; adherence/validation for Agent Skills standard.
vLLM pod manager: setup pods via SSH, start/stop models, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, tool-calling parser presets.
Code search UX: 'file reference' search in editor, fast fuzzy file finder, git-aware ranking, grep integration.
should_have: Web UI components package for chat interfaces and attachments/artifacts workflows; Bun-hosted artifacts server.
Slack bot integration with per-channel storage; recommended sandbox mode.
Observability: structured logs, tracing spans, metrics hooks; redaction and audit trail for sensitive events.
nice_to_have: Extension sandbox tiers (worker/process/WASM) selectable by policy.
Cross-repo search/memory integration (beyond scope of parity).
Built-in provider OAuth flows beyond those needed for initial parity.

non_goals: Guaranteeing drop-in compatibility with every third-party extension without a compatibility layer (best-effort + conformance-driven).
Implementing a full IDE plugin suite (VS Code/JetBrains/etc.).
Building a 'plan mode' or multi-agent orchestration layer as a core feature (pi philosophy keeps core minimal).

behavioral_parity.session_format: 100% read/write compatibility with JSONL v3 sessions (pi-mono docs as oracle).
behavioral_parity.rpc_protocol: >= 95% command parity with pi-mono RPC doc; all P0 commands implemented.
behavioral_parity.tool_conformance: Core tool outputs match fixture-based conformance suite across OS targets.

reliability.crash_free_sessions_rate: >= 99.5% over 7-day rolling window in dogfood telemetry (opt-in)

security.default_policy: Dangerous shell patterns blocked by default unless explicitly allowed.
security.auditability: All tool/exec/http/session/ui hostcalls recorded with redaction and correlate to session + extension identity.

constraints: Primary runtime is Bun; Node compatibility is an open decision.
Must support JSONL v3 session semantics (branching, compaction entries) as specified in pi-mono docs.
Avoid Node-native addons when possible; Bun FFI is experimental and must be treated cautiously.
Slack bot library must be Bun-compatible or implemented via lower-level Slack APIs.

milestones.name: Foundation parity
exit_criteria: JSONL v3 session read/write + tree navigation
Agent loop + tool calls (read/write/edit/bash) with streaming
bun test CI green with coverage

milestones.name: Compatibility surfaces
exit_criteria: RPC mode parity for P0 commands
Package manager + extension hot reload (safe mode)

milestones.name: Ops & ecosystem
exit_criteria: vLLM pods manager parity for setup/start/stop/list/logs
Code search subsystem v1 (fuzzy + grep + git-aware ranking)

deliverables: Monorepo with packages: core, llm, agent, session, tools, extensions, tui, rpc/sdk, pods, slack-bot, web-ui (optional).
spec.md and ADRs for key open decisions.
Conformance + regression test corpus (fixtures) for tools and RPC.
Benchmark harness + baseline results and profiling guide.
Migration guide mapping pi-mono and pi_agent_rust features to new modules.

gates: no known data-loss bugs in session persistence
upgrade path from pi-mono sessions validated

open_decisions: Bun-only vs Bun-first with Node fallback runtime support.
Provider set for GA (which OAuth providers vs API-key-only).
Extension sandbox model: in-process TS, Bun worker isolation, separate process, WASM tier.
Slack integration approach given known Bun runtime issues with some Bolt SDK usage.
Whether to reuse pi-mono subpackages as transitional adapters or reimplement fully with Effect services.
